questions.q: Bhandari committee is related to

options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: For restructuring and strengthening of the RRBs, the government set up the Bhandari Committee (1994-95) and the Basu Committee (1995-96). RRBs were established under RRB Act 1976 on Narasimham Committee (1975) recommendation. Prathama Grameen Bank (2nd Oct 1975) was the 1st RRB, sponsored by Syndicate Bank. Ownership: Central Govt 50%, State Govt 15%, Sponsor Bank 35%. Regulated by RBI, supervised by NABARD.

questions.q: Choose the correct statement related to national pension scheme (NPS)

options.A: NPS is a retirement benefit scheme introduced by the govt of India to facilitate a regular income to all the citizens of India
options.D: NPS is regulated by pension fund regulatory and development authority
options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: NPS is regulated by the Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA). National Pension System Trust is a specialized division of PFRDA, under the jurisdiction of the Ministry of Finance. PFRDA is a statutory body under the PFRDA Act 2013. SEBI (SEBI Act 1992) looks into issues related to security.

questions.q: Which of the following is not included in the priorities of India budget 2022-23?

options.B: Productivity enhancement and investment, sunrise opportunity, energy transition, and climate action
options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: Priorities of Indian budget 2022-23: Inclusive development, Productivity enhancement and investment, Sunrise opportunity, Energy transition, Climate action and PM Gati Shakti. PM Gati Shakti is a national master plan for Multi-modal Connectivity — a digital platform bringing 16 Ministries together for integrated infrastructure planning. Disinvestment is NOT among the stated priorities.

questions.q: What was the tax-GDP ratio in the financial year 2021-2022 in India?

options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: 2021-22 marked the highest tax-GDP ratio of 11.7%, with direct tax-to-GDP ratio at 6.1% and indirect tax-to-GDP ratio at 5.6%. Tax buoyancy was 1.9 (2.8 for direct taxes, 1.1 for indirect taxes). The ratio of direct to indirect taxes recovered from 0.9 in 2020-21 back to 1.1 in 2021-22.

questions.q: What was India's share in world export by commodity division and groups in 2018?

options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: As per WTO data (April 2019), for 2018: India's share in global merchandise exports was 1.7% and in global imports was 2.6%. In services, India's share in global exports was 3.5% and imports 3.2%.

questions.q: What was the production of wheat in India as per the 4th advance estimates in the year 2020-21?

options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: As per 4th Advance Estimates (Dept of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare) for 2020-21: Foodgrains 308.65 MT, Rice 122.27 MT, Wheat 109.52 MT, Nutri/Coarse Cereals 51.15 MT, Maize 31.51 MT, Pulses 25.72 MT, Tur 4.28 MT, Gram 11.99 MT.

questions.q: Choose the correct statement regarding the vibrant village programme.

options.A: It is for the Maharashtra state of India
options.B: It was started in the year 2017
options.C: It is for the development of border village on the northern border
options.D: It is for the development of roads connectivity in Bihar
options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: Vibrant Villages Programme (VVP) was announced in the Finance Minister's Budget Speech 2022-23. It is a centrally sponsored scheme with Rs.4800 Crore allocation (FY 2022-23 to 2025-26) for development of border villages on the northern border with sparse populations, limited connectivity and infrastructure.

questions.q: Which statement is not true about the poverty estimates based on the 68th round of NSSO?

options.A: The poverty line defined at Rs 33.33 per capita per day for urban areas
options.B: The 21.9% population of India was living below the poverty line
options.C: The 33.35% population of India was living below the poverty line
options.D: The poverty line defined at Rs 27.20 per capita per day for rural areas
options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: The percentage of persons below the Poverty Line in 2011-12: 25.7% in rural areas, 13.7% in urban areas, and 21.9% for the country as a whole (Tendulkar Method). India had 270 million persons below poverty line vs 407 million in 2004-05 — a reduction of 137 million. Statement C claiming 33.35% is incorrect.

questions.q: Which of the following partly defines the micro enterprise in India?

options.A: Annual turnover not more than Rs 15 crore
options.B: Investment in plant and machinery not more than Rs 1.5 crore
options.C: Annual turnover of more than Rs 5 crore
options.D: Investment in plant and machinery or equipment not more than Rs 1 crore
options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: Revised Classification w.e.f 1st July 2020 — Micro: Investment not more than Rs.1 crore AND Annual Turnover not more than Rs.5 crores. Small: Investment not more than Rs.10 crore AND Turnover not more than Rs.50 crores. Medium: Investment not more than Rs.50 crore AND Turnover not more than Rs.250 crores.

questions.q:
With reference to the Bare Necessities Index (BNI) consider the following statements:
1. The economic survey has come up with the Bare Necessities Index at rural and urban levels.
2. The BNI has been created for all states for 2018 only.
3. The BNI is based on 26 indicators.
4. The BNI consists of six dimensions.
Which of the above statements are correct?

options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: Economic Survey 2020-21 constructs BNI at rural, urban and all-India level. BNI summarizes 26 indicators on FIVE dimensions: water, sanitation, housing, micro-environment, and other facilities. BNI has been created for all States for 2012 AND 2018 (not 2018 only). Hence statements 1 and 3 are correct; 2 and 4 are wrong.

questions.q: Who among the following is not a member of the 15th finance commission?

options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: The 15th Finance Commission was led by Chairman N.K. Singh. Members: Shri Ajay Narayan Jha, Prof. Anoop Singh, Dr Ashok Lahiri, Dr Ramesh Chand. Secretary: Shri Arvind Mehta. Sudipto Mundle was NOT a member — he is the odd one out.

questions.q: Who is the Chairman of the 15th Finance Commission?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The 15th Finance Commission was led by Chairman Shri. Nand Kishore Singh (N.K. Singh). Members were Shri Ajay Narayan Jha, Prof. Anoop Singh, Dr Ashok Lahiri, and Dr Ramesh Chand.

questions.q: Who was the Chairperson of the Fifteenth Finance Commission?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Fifteenth Finance Commission was led by Chairman Shri. Nand Kishore Singh (N.K. Singh).

questions.q: The best index of economic development is provided by

options.A: Growth in national income at current prices
options.B: Growth in per capita real income from year to year
options.D: Improvement in the balance of payments position
options.E: None of the above / more than one of the above

questions.explanation: Growth in National Income at Current prices includes Tax & subsidy components — if tax increases, it rises without reflecting real development. Growth in savings ratio can be steered by few industrialists. BOP is not a development measure. Growth in per capita real income best reflects actual living standards and development for the common person.

questions.q:
Match List-I with List-II:
List-I: A. Sarkaria Commission  B. C. Rangarajan Committee  C. Parekh Committee  D. Narasimham Committee
List-II: 1. To review the methodology for measurement of poverty  2. Infrastructure Financing  3. Central-State Relationship  4. Banking Sector Reforms

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Sarkaria Commission (1983) — Central-State Relationship (3). C. Rangarajan Committee — Measurement of Poverty (1) (constituted June 2012, submitted 30th June 2014). Parekh Committee — Infrastructure Financing (2) (PPP model for power distribution). Narasimham Committee — Banking Sector Reforms (4).

questions.q: Revenue deficit in India implies that

options.A: The Indian government needs to borrow in order to finance its expenses which will create capital assets.
options.B: The Indian government needs to borrow in order to finance its expenses which do not create capital assets
options.C: The Indian government needs to borrow from the Reserve Bank of India against Government securities
options.D: The Indian government needs to borrow from the international financial institution
options.E: None of the above / more than one

questions.explanation: Revenue Deficit = Revenue Expenditure - Revenue Receipts (when negative). It means the government borrows to finance expenses that do NOT create capital assets. This is considered a bane of the economy as it fails to create assets with a multiplier effect. Art 112 of the Constitution mentions the Annual Financial Statement. Budget 2022-23 of Bihar is a revenue surplus budget (Rs 4747.84 crore = 0.64% of GSDP).

questions.q: Which of the following countries is India's top trading partner?

options.E: None of the above / more than one

questions.explanation: USA was India's top trading partner in 2019-20 (Ministry of Commerce). The US is India's largest market for services exports and overtook China as the largest bilateral trading nation. Recent Data (2021): Top trading countries: USA > China > UAE. Top exporting countries: USA > UAE > China. Top Importing countries: China > USA > UAE.

questions.q: Which of the following infrastructure sectors of India is related to Bharatmala Project?

options.E: None of the above / more than one

questions.explanation: Bharatmala Project is related to the Ministry of Road Transport & Highways. It focuses on enhanced effectiveness of existing infrastructure, multi-modal integration, bridging infrastructure gaps for seamless movement, and integrating National and Economic Corridors. India has about 63.31 lakh km of road network — the second largest in the world (2019 data).

questions.q: Which of the following agencies releases the index of industrial production to measure industrial performance in India?

options.E: None of the above / More than one

questions.explanation: IIP is compiled and published monthly by the National Statistical Office (NSO), Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation. NSSO merged with CSO to form NSO (approved 23rd May 2019). IIP contains 8 core sectors comprising 40.27% of its weight. Eight core sectors (decreasing weightage): Refinery Products > Electricity > Steel > Coal > Crude Oil > Natural Gas > Cement > Fertilizers. Base year: 2011-12.

questions.q: Which of the following is not the method to calculate the Gross Domestic Product (GDP)?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: GDP can be measured in three ways: (1) Value-added approach (Product method); (2) Income approach; (3) Expenditures approach. 'Diminishing cost method' does not exist as a method of calculating GDP.

questions.q: Which of the following offices or institutes releases data on the national income in India?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The National Statistical Office (NSO) under MoSPI releases the Estimates of National Income. The government merged NSSO with CSO (order dated 23rd May 2019) to form the NSO under MoSPI.

questions.q: When was gender budgeting initiated in India?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Gender Budgeting is a tool for achieving gender mainstreaming ensuring development benefits reach women as much as men. Initiated in India in Union Budget 2005-06. Australia was the 1st country to introduce Gender Budgeting in 1984.

questions.q:
Lowering the cash reserve ratio will have the following impact on the economy:
I. Banks will have higher leverage to liquidity.
II. The economy may see increased investment.
III. The supply of currency in the economy may broaden.
IV. The real investment rate may decline.
Select the correct code:

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: CRR is the proportion of deposits banks keep with RBI as liquid cash. When RBI slashes CRR, banks get more money to lend (I), leading to more investment (II) and broader money supply (III). The Commission went with all four statements. Note: Ideally I, II, III should be the answer but BPSC selected C.

questions.q: Which one of the following is likely to be the most inflationary in its effects?

options.B: Borrowing from The Public to Finance Budget Deficit
options.C: Borrowing from The Banks to Finance Budget Deficit
options.D: Creation of New Money to Finance Budget Deficit

questions.explanation: Creation of new money to finance budget deficit is the most inflationary because it directly increases the money supply without any corresponding increase in goods and services, leading to demand-pull inflation.

questions.q: Which one of the following is likely to be the most inflationary in its effect?

options.B: Borrowing from The Public to Finance a Budget Deficit
options.C: Borrowing from Banks to Finance a Budget Deficit
options.D: Creating New Money to Finance a Budget Deficit

questions.explanation: Creating new money directly increases money supply without corresponding goods production, making it the most inflationary option.

questions.q: With reference to inflation in India, which of the following statements is correct?

options.A: Controlling the Inflation in India Is the Responsibility of the Government of India Only
options.B: The Reserve Bank of India Has No Role in Controlling the Inflation
options.C: Decreased Money Circulation Helps in Controlling the Inflation
options.D: Increased Money Circulation Helps in Controlling the Inflation

questions.explanation: Decreased money circulation helps control inflation. When RBI raises CRR, banks have less money to lend, reducing money supply and controlling inflation. RBI plays a key role through monetary policy tools (CRR, SLR, Repo rate, etc.).

questions.q: What is the share of Bihar in the divisible pool of Central Taxes on the recommendation of the 15th Finance Commission?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The share of Bihar in the divisible pool of Central Taxes per 15th Finance Commission is 10.06%. The share of states in central taxes for 2021-26 is 41% (same as 2020-21), less than the 42% of 14th Finance Commission — the 1% adjustment is for the newly formed UTs of J&K and Ladakh.

questions.q: Which one of the following is not a source of direct finance?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: NABARD is the apex regulatory body for RRBs and apex cooperative banks. It provides finance THROUGH cooperative and commercial banks and RRBs — making it an indirect source, not a direct source. NABARD was established 12 July 1982 on recommendations of B. Sivaraman Committee.

questions.q: The apex bank for providing agricultural refinance in India is

options.E: None of these / More than one of these

questions.explanation: NABARD is the apex regulatory body for overall regulation of regional rural banks and apex cooperative banks in India. It provides investment and production credit for developmental activities in rural areas.

questions.q: Which of the following institutions is the apex institution at the national level for agricultural credit?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: NABARD (National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development) is the apex institution at the national level for agricultural credit. It provides refinance to commercial banks, RRBs and cooperative banks for agricultural lending.

questions.q: What is the full form of EPCG?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: EPCG stands for Export Promotion Capital Goods Scheme — a programme by the Indian government to encourage exports by allowing import of capital items needed in pre-production, manufacturing, and post-production without paying customs tax.

questions.q: Who determines the Minimum Support Price in India?

options.A: The Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices
options.B: The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: MSPs are announced by the Government on recommendations of the Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP), an attached office of the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, since January 1965. CACP recommends MSPs for 23 commodities (7 cereals, 5 pulses, 7 oilseeds, 4 commercial crops). MSP was first mooted in 1966-67. Strictly, CACP recommends but Cabinet decides — BPSC went with option A.

questions.q: Which one of the following decides the minimum support prices on behalf of the Government of India?

options.D: Commission for agricultural cost and prices
options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) recommends the MSP for 23 commodities. It is an attached office of the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, operating since January 1965.

questions.q: Hindu growth rate is related to

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Hindu growth rate is related to GDP. The term was coined by Professor Rajkrishna in 1978 to denote the slow growth rate of the Indian economy before liberalization of 1991 (from the 1950s to the 1980s), which averaged around 4%. The Indian economy of this period is characterized as Dirigisme.

questions.q: Which one of the following programmes was initiated during the Sixth Five-year Plan?

options.D: Advanced Communication Links for Rural People
options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Integrated Rural Development Programme (IRDP) was launched in 1978-79 and put into effect in 1980. DWCRA was launched in 1982 as part of IRDP. IRDP was the largest programme of the Sixth Five Year Plan (1980-85) for alleviation of poverty in rural areas.

questions.q: During the Twelfth Five-Year Plan, which one of the following average annual growth rate targets was envisaged for agriculture and allied sector?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The 12th Five-Year Plan (2012-17) was India's last Five-Year Plan. Its agriculture growth target was 4.0% per annum. Deputy Chairman: Mr Montek Singh Ahluwalia. Planning Commission was dissolved into NITI Aayog on 1 Jan 2015.

questions.q: The First Five-Year Plan of India was based on the

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The First Five-Year Plan (1951-56) presented by Jawaharlal Nehru was based on the Harrod-Domar Model, which stresses savings and investment as key determinants of growth. It addressed the agrarian sector. Target growth: 2.1%, Achieved: 3.6%. Three major hydroelectric projects: Bhakra Nangal, Hirakud, Nagarjuna Sagar Dam. FYP concept was taken from erstwhile USSR.

questions.q: Which of the following were treated as separate Annual Plans?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: 1990-91 and 1991-92 were treated as separate Annual Plans due to political uncertainty and the economic crisis. The 8th Five Year Plan was subsequently launched in 1992.

questions.q: Antyodaya Programme was started first of all in the State of

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Antyodaya programme was launched first by the Government of Rajasthan in 1977 for upliftment of the poorest of the poor. Then taken up by HP, Orissa, UP, Bihar, and Manipur in 1978. 'Azadi Se Antyodaya Tak' is a 90-day campaign launched on 28th April 2022 led by the Ministry of Rural Development.

questions.q: In the Government of India's NITI Aayog, which is an economic policy-making think tank, what is the full form of NITI?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: NITI = National Institution for Transforming India. NITI Aayog is a premiere policy Think Tank established in 2015 by executive resolution replacing the Planning Commission. Works on Bottom to Top approach (Planning Commission was Top to Bottom). PM is Ex-officio Chairman. 1st Vice Chairman: Arvind Pangariya. 1st CEO: Sindhushree Khullar. Present Vice Chairman: Mr. Suman Bery (since 1 May 2022). CEO: Shri B.V.R. Subrahmanyam (since 25 Feb 2023). 5-year plan replaced by 15-year Vision Document.

questions.q: NITI Aayog came into existence on

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: NITI Aayog came into existence on 1st January 2015, replacing the Planning Commission of India via an executive resolution.

questions.q: NITI Aayog was established in 2015 in place of Planning Commission which was dissolved in

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: In August 2014, the Prime Minister announced that the Planning Commission would be abolished and a Niti Aayog would be set up in its place. The Planning Commission was dissolved in 2014 and NITI Aayog formally came into existence on 1st January 2015.

questions.q: Who among the following has given the idea of Self-Help Groups as an effective tool for poverty alleviation?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Muhammad Yunus is a Bangladeshi social entrepreneur who won the Nobel Peace Prize for founding the Grameen Bank and pioneering microcredit and microfinance. Amartya Sen: Indian Economist, theory of Human Development, Nobel Prize in Economics 1998, Bharat Ratna 1999. S. Chakravarti (Sukhamoy Chakravarty): key architect of India's Five-Year Plans along with Mahalanobis.

questions.q: Which one of the following is the objective of MGNREGA?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: MGNREGA (passed 23 Aug 2005, implemented Feb 2006 under PM Manmohan Singh). Aim: Provide 100 days of guaranteed wage employment (unskilled manual work) AND create durable assets (roads, canals, ponds, wells). It is an initiative of the Ministry of Rural Development. Since both A and D are partially correct objectives but no single option captures all, the answer is E.

questions.q: Which one of the following most appropriately describes the nature of the Green Revolution of the late sixties of the 20th century?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Green Revolution began in India in the 1960s using Seed-Fertiliser-Water technology (HYV seeds, mechanised tools, irrigation, pesticides, fertilizers). Brainchild of Norman Borlaug (Nobel Peace Prize 1970, Padma Vibhushan 2006). Led in India by M.S. Swaminathan (first World Food Prize 1987; also awarded Padma Shri, Padma Bhushan, Padma Vibhushan).

questions.q: One of the problems in calculating the National Income in India is

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The non-organised sector is outside the taxation system. Its transactions remain unaccounted for, making it a major problem in calculating National Income in India.

questions.q: Which sector in India attracts the highest FDI equity flow?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Computer Software and Hardware (Service Sector) is the top recipient sector of FDI Equity inflow with ~25% share. Top FDI equity inflows: Singapore (27%), followed by USA (18%). Karnataka was the top FDI equity inflow recipient state in India (FY 2020-21).

questions.q: Which of the following States of India Received the highest Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) equity flow during April to September 2022?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: India recorded highest-ever annual FDI inflow of USD 83.57 billion in FY 2021-22. Karnataka topped the FDI list for 2021-22 attracting Rs 1,62,830 crore, followed by Maharashtra at Rs 1,14,964 crore. Top FDI equity inflows: Singapore (27%), USA (18%). Computer Software and Hardware is the top recipient sector (~25% share).

questions.q: The size of marginal land holdings in India is

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Agriculture census classification of operational holdings: Marginal — Below 1.00 hectare; Small — 1.00-2.00 hectare; Semi-medium — 2.00-4.00 hectare; Medium — 4.00-10.00 hectare; Large — 10.00 hectare and above.

questions.q: Globalisation does not include

options.E: none of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Globalisation refers to the process by which businesses develop international influence. Reduction in import duties, abolition of import licensing, and free flow of FDI are all parts of globalisation. Disinvestment of Public Sector Equity is part of liberalisation of the economy — not globalisation.

questions.q: The Prevention of Money Laundering Act came into force in India during

options.E: None of these / More than one of these

questions.explanation: Prevention of Money Laundering Act, 2002 was enacted during the NDA government to prevent money laundering and provide for confiscation of property derived from money laundering. It came into force from 1st July 2005.

questions.q: TRIMS stands for

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: TRIMS stands for Trade-Related Investment Measures. The WTO Agreement on TRIMs is based on the belief that restrictive measures on investment are trade distorting. Countries should not adopt investment measures that restrict and distort trade.

questions.q: The base year for All-India Wholesale Price Index (WPI) has been changed by the Government of India from 2004-05 to

options.E: None of these / More than one of these

questions.explanation: WPI is computed by the Office of Economic Advisor, Dept of Commerce, on the basis of 697 items (Manufacturing, Fuel and light, Primary goods). Nearly 65% weight is assigned to manufacturing goods. Base year changed to 2011-12 on 13th May 2017.

questions.q: Which one of the following approaches is associated with Prof. Amartya Sen?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Capability Approach given by Amartya Sen focuses on the moral significance of individuals' capability of achieving the kind of lives they have reason to value — defined in terms of the set of valuable 'beings and doings' (being in good health, having loving relationships, etc.) to which they have real access.

questions.q: The 'Ease of Doing Business Index' is prepared and published by

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The 'Ease of Doing Business Index' is prepared and published by the World Bank Group since 2003. In 2021, the World Bank discontinued this report due to lack of transparency and data manipulation findings. According to 2019 report, India was ranked 63 among 190 economies. World Bank also releases: World Development Report, Remittance Report, Global Economic Prospect Report.

questions.q: Which one of the following measures is not likely to aid in improving India's Balance of Payment position?

options.C: Imposition of higher tariff on imports
options.D: Levying of higher duties on exports
options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Improving BOP means improving exports and reducing imports. Levying higher duties on exports will DISCOURAGE exports — this harms BOP. Promotion of import substitution and higher import tariffs reduce imports (improve BOP). Devaluation discourages imports and encourages exports (improves BOP). Hence D does not aid BOP improvement.

questions.q: The balance of payment of a country is a systematic record of

options.A: All import and export transactions of a country during a given period of time, normally a year
options.B: goods exported from a country during a year
options.C: Economic transaction between the government of one country to another
options.D: Capital movements from one country to another

questions.explanation: BOP is a statement of all transactions made between entities in one country and the rest of the world over a defined period. It includes both the current account (trade in goods and services) and the capital account (capital flows).

questions.q: The economist who for the first time scientifically determined National Income in India:

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: V.K.R.V. Rao first scientifically determined National Income in India — published 'An Essay on India's National Income, 1925-29' in 1934. He is the father of National Income accounting in India. Dadabhai Naoroji (1867-68): first attempt (estimated per capita income at Rs 20). First official attempt: National Income Committee headed by Prof. PC Mahalanobis in 1949.

questions.q: Service tax was introduced in India on the recommendation of:

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Service tax was introduced on the recommendation of Raja J. Chelliah's Committee on Tax Reforms (appointed 1991). GST idea was first mooted in 2002 by Vijay Kelkar Task Force. Kelkar Committee: private investment in infrastructure (PPP model). Yashwant Sinha Committee: tax reforms and GST.

questions.q: Which of the following Tax is levied by the Union and collected and appropriated by States?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Stamp duties are levied by the Union and collected and appropriated by States. Taxes in India fall into six groups including: Levied by Centre but Collected and Appropriated by States (which includes stamp duties).

questions.q: The controlling authority of government expenditure is
questions.explanation: The Controlling Authority is the Finance Ministry via RBI. RBI is not completely independent — it acts as per the RBI Act and rules framed by Parliament (Ministry of Finance) from time to time.

questions.q: Effective Revenue Deficit was introduced in the Union Budget of
questions.explanation: Effective Revenue Deficit was introduced in Union Budget 2011-12, prescribed by an amendment to the FRBM Act by Finance Act 2012. It is defined as: Revenue Deficit minus Grants for creation of capital assets. This concept was suggested by the Rangarajan Committee on Public Expenditure.

questions.q: Interest payment is an item of
questions.explanation: Interest expense is the cost incurred for borrowed funds — interest payable on bonds, loans, convertible debt or lines of credit. It is classified as Revenue Expenditure.

questions.q: Foreign currency which has a tendency of quick migration is called
questions.explanation: Hot currency (hot money) quickly and regularly moves between financial markets to lock in the highest available short-term interest rates. It continuously shifts from countries with low-interest rates to those with higher rates.

questions.q: Which one of the following is not included in the National Food Security Missions?
questions.explanation: National Food Security Mission (NFSM) was launched in 2007-08 to increase production of rice, wheat and pulses. Coarse cereals were added from 2014-15. Oilseeds are NOT included in NFSM.

questions.q: RN Malhotra Committee is associated with
questions.explanation: In 1993, the Govt. of India set up a committee under R.N. Malhotra, former Governor of RBI, to propose recommendations for reforms in the insurance sector.

questions.q: Service tax in India was introduced in the year
questions.explanation: Service Tax was brought into force from July 1, 1994, vide chapter V of the Finance Act, 1994. Post GST (1 July 2017), service tax was subsumed in GST.

questions.q: Convertibility of the rupee implies

options.A: being able to convert rupee notes into gold
options.B: freely permitting the conversion of rupee to other major currencies and vice versa
options.C: allowing the value of the rupee to be fixed by market forces
options.D: developing an international market for currencies in India

questions.explanation: Convertibility is the ease with which a currency can be converted into other currencies through global exchanges. India's rupee is partially convertible — can be exchanged at market rates in certain cases, but approval is required for larger amounts.

questions.q: What does the infant mortality rate refer to?

options.A: The ratio of number of children who die before their fifth birthday out of every 1000 live births
options.B: The ratio of number of children who die before their first birthday out of every 1000 live births
options.C: The ratio of number of children who die before their fifth birthday out of every 100 live births
options.D: The ratio of number of children who die before their first birthday out of every 100 live births

questions.explanation: Infant mortality rate = ratio of children who die before their FIRST birthday out of every 1000 live births. As per SRS Bulletin of RGI, IMR reduced from 37 per 1000 live births in 2015 to 30 per 1,000 live births in 2019.

questions.q: In which of the following years was the trade balance favourable to India?
questions.explanation: A positive trade balance (favourable) means a country is a net exporter. In 1972-73 and 1976-77, India had a favourable trade balance.

questions.q: During which year was the annual average growth rate (at constant prices) of agriculture and allied sectors negative?
questions.explanation: The annual average growth rate of agriculture and allied sectors was negative in 2002-03 due to the severe drought that year.

questions.q: Bank rate means

options.B: Interest rate charged by Scheduled Banks
options.C: Rate of profit of banking institution
options.D: Official rate of interest charged by Central Bank
options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The bank rate is the rate at which RBI provides loans to commercial banks without keeping any security — used for long-term lending. Repo rate is similar but for short-term lending with a repurchase agreement. MPC (statutory body under Section 45ZB of RBI Act 1934) determines the policy repo rate; headed by RBI Governor with 6 members; meets at least 4 times a year.

questions.q: An increase in the Bank Rate generally indicates that the

options.A: Market Rate of Interest Is Likely to Fall
options.B: Central Bank Is No Longer Making Loans to Commercial Banks
options.C: Central Bank Is Following an Easy Money Policy
options.D: Central Bank Is Following a Tight Money Policy

questions.explanation: An increase in the Bank Rate indicates the Central Bank is following a tight money policy. Higher bank rates translate to higher lending rates, reducing borrowing and money supply — used to control inflation.

questions.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. The bank rate is the rate of interest that RBI charges its clients on their short-term borrowing.
2. Repo rate is the rate of interest that RBI charges its clients on their long-term borrowing.
Which of the statements given above is/are incorrect?
questions.explanation: Both statements are INCORRECT. Bank rate is for LONG-term loans (not short-term). Repo rate is for SHORT-term lending (not long-term). Repo rate: a system where the loan is available for a short duration, used to control inflation.

questions.q: Which of the following is not a quantitative method of credit control?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Quantitative methods of credit control: Bank rate, Repo rate, CRR, SLR, Open Market Operations. Margin Requirements is a QUALITATIVE (selective) method of credit control — NOT quantitative. Margin requirement = difference between the current value of the security (collateral) and the loan value granted.

questions.q: Financial sector reforms in India consist of

options.A: lowering down of CRR and SLR
options.B: entry of private firms in insurance sector

questions.explanation: Financial sector reforms in India include: Reduction in SLR and CRR; End of Administered Interest Rate Regime; Competitive Financial System; Elimination of Direct Credit Controls; Promoting Microfinance for Financial Inclusion; Setting up Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF); Entry of private firms in the insurance sector.

questions.q: Who among the following heads the expert committee for studying the advisability of granting new urban Cooperative banking licences (UCBs)?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Y.H. Malegam heads the expert committee for granting new UCB licences. UCBs are primary cooperative banks in urban and semi-urban areas. Till 1996, they lent money only for non-agricultural purposes. They essentially lend to small borrowers and businesses.

questions.q: From time to time, which among the following publishes the 'exchange control manual' in context with the foreign exchange in India?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: RBI publishes the 'exchange control manual' in context with the foreign exchange in India.

questions.q: Which of the following organisations provides a guarantee to exporters in India?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: ECGC Ltd. (Export Credit Guarantee Corporation of India Ltd.) provides a guarantee to exporters. It is wholly owned by the Government of India, set up in 1957 to promote exports by providing credit risk insurance and related services.

questions.q: Which agency is responsible for procurement, distribution and food grain production in India?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Food Corporation of India was set up under the Food Corporations of India Act 1964 to: provide effective price support operations for farmers; distribute food grains for the Public Distribution System; and maintain satisfactory operational and buffer stocks of foodgrains for National Food Security.

questions.q: Which among the following is called the financial backbone of the Indian power sector?

options.C: Power Grid Corporation of India Limited
options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Power Finance Corporation (PFC) is a central public sector undertaking under the Ministry of Power. Established in 1986, it is the financial backbone of the Indian Power Sector. Conferred 'Maharatna CPSE' in Oct. 2021 and classified as Infrastructure Finance Company by RBI on 28th July 2010.

questions.q: When the supply for money increases and the demand for money reduces, will there be?

options.A: A decrease in rate of interest
options.B: A fall in the level of prices
options.C: A fall in level of demand
options.D: An increase in rate of interest
options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: When money supply increases and demand for money reduces, there will be a decrease in the rate of interest. Higher supply with lower demand means money is more freely available, pushing down the cost of borrowing (interest rates).

questions.q: Which ministry is responsible for calculating GDP of India?

options.A: The ministry of Commerce and industry
options.D: Ministry of Central statistical and programme implementation
options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) is responsible for calculating GDP of India. It came into existence on 15.10.1999 after the merger of the Department of Statistics and the Department of Programme Implementation.

questions.q: Which Committee recommended the Interim Liquidity Adjustment Facility (ILAF)?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Narasimham Committee Report on Banking Reforms (Narasimham Committee II) recommended introducing a Liquidity Adjustment Facility (LAF) operated through repo and reverse repo. LAF is a monetary policy tool used by RBI allowing banks to borrow through repurchase agreements or lend to RBI through reverse repo agreements.

questions.q: The 'Special Economic Zones' were modelled on the lines of a highly successful experiment.

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: SEZ Scheme in India was conceived by Commerce and Industries Minister Murasoli Maran during a visit to SEZs in China in 1999. Announced at the annual review of the EXIM Policy from 1.4.2000. SEZ is a specifically delineated duty-free enclave deemed to be a foreign territory for trade operations.

questions.q:
Which of the following are the objectives of the SEZ Act, 2005?
1. Generation of additional economic activity
2. Promotion of exports of goods and services
3. Creation of employment

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Objectives of SEZ Act 2005 include: generation of additional economic activity, promotion of exports of goods and services, promotion of investment from domestic and foreign sources, creation of employment opportunities, and development of infrastructure facilities. All three stated objectives are correct.

questions.q: India joined the World Trade Organization in

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: India has been a WTO member since 1 January 1995 and a member of GATT since 8 July 1948. WTO is an intergovernmental organization that regulates and facilitates international trade. Headquarters: Geneva, Switzerland. Members: 164.

questions.q: MODVAT is related to

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: MODVAT (Modified Value Added Tax) was incorporated into the Indian tax system in 1986, related to excise duty. It provided excise credit to manufacturers on raw materials, eliminating double taxation in the excise duty chain.

questions.q: NAFED is a/an

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: National Agricultural Cooperative Marketing Federation of India Ltd. (NAFED) was established on 2nd October 1958, registered under the Multi-State Co-operative Societies Act. Set up to promote cooperative marketing of agricultural produce. Agricultural farmers are the main members.

questions.q: Inequality of income is measured by

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Gini index or Gini coefficient was developed by Italian statistician Corrado Gini in 1912. It measures income distribution among a population. Maximum value of 1 = complete inequality; minimum value of 0 = complete equality. Value >= 0.25 is considered alarming.

questions.q: Due to the oil crisis, most of the developed countries faced the problem of

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Stagflation = Stagnation + Inflation. The OPEC's fourfold increase in oil prices in 1973 raised all prices, slowing economic growth while keeping inflation high — classic stagflation. A deadly condition that can only be cured through major structural reforms.

questions.q: Who wrote the book Indian Economic Crisis?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: C.T. Kurien is the author of the book 'Indian Economic Crisis'.

questions.q:
Which of the following investors/agencies can purchase Government of India securities?
I. The Reserve Bank of India
II. Individuals
III. Financial Institutions
Select the correct answer using the code given below.

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Government Securities (G-Secs) are tradable instruments issued by the Central/State Governments — short-term (treasury bills, maturity <1 year) or long-term (Government bonds, maturity >=1 year). They can be purchased by the RBI, Individuals, and Financial Institutions.

questions.q: How many times does the Reserve Bank of India review the Monetary Policy in a financial year?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: MPC meets once in 2 months (bimonthly), mandated to meet at least 4 times a year. Quorum for MPC: four members. Each member has one vote; in case of equality, the Governor has a casting vote. MPC is a statutory body under Section 45ZB of RBI Act 1934, headed by RBI Governor with 6 members. Objective: keep inflation within (4±2)%.

questions.q: The Reserve Bank of India is using which of the following inflation indices as an anchor for 'Inflation Targeting'?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Central Government, in consultation with RBI, determines the inflation target in terms of Consumer Price Index (CPI)-combined, once in five years, notified in the Official Gazette. The target is to keep inflation within (4±2)%.

questions.q: As per the new methodology, the lending rate is calculated by the banks based on

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: RBI instituted the Marginal Cost of Funds-based Lending Rate (MCLR) system effective April 1, 2016. It is the lowest rate of fund lending by banks, calculated internally. Objectives: improve policy rate transmission, bring transparency, make banks more competitive, ensure fair credit at the right rate.

questions.q: The amount of money supply in the economy affects which of the following macroeconomic variables?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Changes in money supply affect the supply-demand scenario in the market, which impacts Price level, Rate of interest, Employment, and Output. Since all options A, B, C, D are affected by money supply changes, the answer is E (more than one of the above).

questions.q: The visit of foreigners in India to see the various places or events in the country amounts to which of the following in terms of economy?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Visiting foreigners bring foreign currency (dollars) into India and utilize Indian services. This is counted as invisible exports — receipts from tourism are export earnings for India.

questions.q: The grants-in-aid given by the Central Government to the State Government and local bodies for creation of capital assets are classified in the Union Budget under

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: All grants given to State Governments/UTs and other parties are treated as Revenue Expenditure even though some grants may be used for creation of assets. Revenue expenditure broadly means expenditure that does not result in creation of assets for the Government of India.

questions.q: The money accruing from the sale of 'National Savings Certificates' goes to which account of the Government of India?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Article 266 of the Constitution defines the Public Account as funds received on behalf of the Government. NSC proceeds (Small Savings), Provident Funds, income set apart for specific objects are kept in the Public Account. These funds do not belong to the Government and must be paid back to the depositors.

questions.q: Fiscal deficit of the Government of India is equal to which of the following?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Fiscal Deficit = Total Expenditure - (Revenue Receipts + Non-debt capital Receipts). This means Fiscal Deficit = Debt-creating capital receipts (borrowings). Primary deficit = Gross fiscal deficit - Net interest liabilities.

questions.q: What does 'Revenue Neutral Tax Rate' mean in reference to 'Goods and Services Tax (GST)'?

options.A: The rate at which tax revenues for States and Centre will remain the same as before GST
options.B: The tax rate will be same for the Centre and States
options.C: The tax rate at which Central and State revenues will be same
options.D: Proportion of tax revenue of States to Centre should increase
options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: 'Revenue Neutral Tax Rate' means the rate at which tax revenues for States and Centre will remain the same as before GST. As per RBI, the revenue neutral rate is 15%, but the average of different GST slabs is 11.5% — showing a slight mismatch.

questions.q: In India, the agricultural 'crop year' period is

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: In India, the agricultural crop year period is 1st July to 30th June. A crop year is a period from one year's harvest to the next for an agricultural commodity.

questions.q: Currency notes of Rs. 2 denomination and above are liabilities of the

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Currency notes of Rs. 2 denominations and above are liabilities of the Reserve Bank of India. Rupee 1 coin and note is the liability of the Government of India (through the Ministry of Finance).

questions.q: Crowding effect is a phenomenon that occurs when increased government involvement in a sector of market economy effects remainder of the market on

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Crowding out effect refers to reduced private borrowing due to huge Government borrowing. If Government spending goes directly to citizens (e.g., PMKISAN), it affects the demand side. If invested in capital expenditure, it affects the supply side. So the effect is Either A or B — answer is D.

questions.q: If the implicit price of GDP in 2015-16 is assumed as base year, which of the following years have deflator value as 100?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: GDP Deflator = (Nominal GDP / Real GDP) x 100. When the base year (2015-16) is also the nominal year, nominal and real GDP are equal, so deflator = 100. Since none of the options given (2016-17, 2017-18, 2018-19, 2019-20) is 2015-16, the answer is E (None of the above).

questions.q: In some countries, GST is also known as

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: GST is also known as Value Added Tax (VAT) in some countries. It is a multi-stage, destination-based tax levied on the value added at each stage of the supply chain.

questions.q: The Gandhian Plan was formulated by

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: In 1944, the Gandhian Plan was formulated by Sriman Narayan Agarwal. It emphasized promotion of small-unit production and agriculture based on Gandhian economic ideas. Key characteristic: economic decentralization, with self-contained villages and cottage enterprises.

questions.q: The transformation of taxes on domestic products into the VAT was first recommended by

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The transformation of taxes on domestic products into VAT was first recommended by the Raja Chelliah Committee. Set up in August 1991 under Dr Raja J Chelliah to recommend a comprehensive reform of the central tax system — primarily reforming direct and indirect taxes.

questions.q: Which of the following indicators is essential to calculate the Human Development Index?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: HDI (released by UNDP) considers three indicators: (1) Life expectancy, (2) Education (both expected years of schooling and mean years of schooling), (3) Per capita income (GNI per capita). Since options A, B, C, and D are ALL essential for HDI, the answer is E (more than one of the above).

questions.q: The DPIIT, under the Ministry of Commerce and Trade, was established in

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) was established in 1995, reconstituted in 2000 with merger of the Department of Industrial Development. Earlier called Department of Industrial Policy & Promotion, renamed DPIIT in January 2019.

questions.q: The base year of Index of Core Industries (ICI) has recently been changed to

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Office of Economic Adviser within DPIIT releases the Index of Eight Core Industries (ICI). The eight core industries measured: coal, crude oil, natural gas, refinery products, fertilizers, steel, cement and electricity. Base year has been changed to 2011-12.

questions.q: Who was the first Finance Minister of Independent India?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Sir R.K. Shanmukham Chetty was the first Finance Minister of India (1947-1948). He also served as President of India's Central Legislative Assembly (1933-1935) and Diwan of Cochin Kingdom (1935-1941).

questions.q: Annual Financial Statements and Finance Bill are presented before the Parliament under the Constitutional Articles respectively.

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Annual Financial Statement is presented under Article 112 of the Constitution. Finance Bill is a Money Bill defined under Article 110 of the Constitution. Hence the pair is Article 112 (AFS) and Article 110 (Finance Bill) — answer D.

questions.q: Which of the following was the first country to implement GST in 1954?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: France was the first country to implement GST in 1954. China implemented GST in 1994 and Russia in 1991. GST came into effect on 1 July 2017 in India.

questions.q: Competition Commission India Act was passed in

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Competition Commission of India (CCI) was established under the Competition Act, 2002, and constituted in March 2009. Objectives: prevent practices having adverse effects on competition, promote and sustain competition in markets, protect consumer interests, and ensure freedom of trade.

questions.q: Railway Budget was merged with the General Budget in

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: In 2017, the Railway Budget was merged with the Union Budget, ending a practice that began in 1924 under the British. A NITI Aayog commission recommended abolishing the practice, submitted to Railway Minister Suresh Prabhu. The merger happened in Union Budget 2017-18.

questions.q: Who among the following became the first Woman Finance Minister of India?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was the first Woman Finance Minister of India. She took over the Finance portfolio in July 1969 after Morarji Desai's resignation following nationalization of major banks. Nirmala Sitharaman is the first FULL-TIME female Finance Minister.

questions.q: ESIC Act was passed in

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: ESIC (Employees' State Insurance Corporation) Act was passed in 1948. It provides for certain benefits to employees in case of sickness, maternity and employment injury, and other related matters.

questions.q: The book, 'Walking with the Comrades' is written by

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: 'Walking with the Comrades' is written by Arundhati Roy. It covers her time in 2010 spent with the Naxalite communities of Chhattisgarh.

questions.q: Who developed harmonised system, a six-digit harmonised code?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The World Customs Organization's Harmonized System (HS) uses six-digit code numbers to define and classify traded products internationally.

questions.q: What is the full form of TRP used in TV channels?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: TRP stands for Television Rating Point. It is a tool used to measure the popularity of a television channel or programme.

questions.q: Which of the following committees was set up by the government, which suggested how to double farmers' income?

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Ashok Dalwai Committee was set up to suggest ways to double farmer income (target year: 2022). Other committees: Shanta Kumar Committee — Restructuring FCI; Rangarajan Committee — Poverty; Urjit Patel Committee — Monetary policy framework.

questions.q: The Industrial Policy Resolution of 1948 proposed

options.E: None of the above / More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Industrial Policy Resolution of 1948 proposed a Mixed economy — combining aspects of both capitalism and socialism. It protects private property and allows economic freedom in use of capital, while also allowing government interference in economic activities to achieve social aims.

questions.q: The money multiplier in an economy increases with which one of the following?

options.A: Increase in The Cash Reserve Ratio in The Banks
options.B: Increase in The Statutory Liquidity Ratio in The Banks.
options.C: Increase in The Banking Habit of The People.
options.D: Increase in The Population of The Country.

questions.explanation: Money Multiplier = 1/CRR. Higher reserve ratio (CRR or SLR) = fewer deposits for lending = lower multiplier — so A and B are incorrect. More banking habit = more deposits with banks = more lending = higher multiplier. Population increase alone has no direct relation with multiplier. Hence C is correct.

questions.q: Priority Sector Lending by banks in India constitutes the lending to
questions.explanation: PSL mandates scheduled commercial banks to set aside 40% of ANBC for: Agriculture, MSMEs, Export Credit, Education, Housing, Social Infrastructure, Renewable Energy, Startups, and others. RRBs, cooperative banks and small finance banks must allocate 75% to PSL. Banks falling short must invest in NABARD (scheduled commercial) or SIDBI (foreign banks).

questions.q: The national income of a country for a given period is equal to the

options.A: Total Value of Goods and Services Produced by The Nationals
options.B: Sum of Total Consumption and Investment Expenditure
options.C: Sum of Personal Income of All Individuals
options.D: Money Value of Final Goods and Services Produced.

questions.explanation: National income = sum total of incomes earned by normal residents of that country during a given period. GNP = GDP + Net factor income from abroad. For countries like India and China, GNP is usually less than GDP as net factor income from abroad is negative.

questions.q: Disguised unemployment generally means

options.A: Large Number of People Remain Unemployed
options.C: Marginal Productivity of Labor Is Zero

questions.explanation: Disguised unemployment means more people are engaged in a job that can be done with fewer people — extra people can be removed without affecting productivity, i.e., their marginal productivity is zero. Commonly seen in agriculture (e.g., whole family working on the same farm).

questions.q: In the context of Indian economy, 'Open Market Operations' refers to

options.A: Borrowing by Scheduled Banks from the RBI
options.B: Lending by Commercial Banks to Industry and Trade
options.C: Purchase and Sale of Government Securities by the RBI

questions.explanation: Open Market Operations (OMO) = sale and purchase of government securities and treasury bills by RBI through open market auctions (done quarterly). When RBI wants to increase money supply, it purchases securities (Quantitative easing). To suck out liquidity, it sells securities. OMO is a medium-term liquidity adjustment facility.

questions.q: The main objective of the 12th Five-Year Plan is

options.C: Sustainable and Inclusive Growth to Reduce Unemployment
options.D: Faster, Sustainable and More Inclusive Growth.

questions.explanation: The 12th Five-Year Plan (2012-17) under Manmohan Singh had the theme: 'Faster, Sustainable, and More Inclusive Growth.' Objectives: 8% real GDP growth per year, agriculture at 4%, manufacturing at 10%, and every state to outperform the 11th plan.

questions.q: If the interest rate is decreased in an economy, it will

options.A: Decrease the Consumption Expenditure in The Economy
options.B: Increase the Tax Collection of The Government
options.C: Increase the Investment Expenditure in The Economy
options.D: Increase the Total Savings in The Economy

questions.explanation: When interest rates decrease: money is more easily available, so Investment in industries and housing increases (C is correct). Consumption also increases (A is incorrect — it decreases consumption is wrong). Savings decrease (D is incorrect — lower rates reduce incentive to save). Tax collection has no direct immediate link.

questions.q: Convertibility of rupee implies:

options.A: Being able to Convert Rupee Notes into Gold
options.B: Allowing the Value of Rupee to Be Fixed by Market Forces
options.C: Freely Permitting the Conversion of Rupee to Other Currencies and Vice Versa
options.D: Developing an International Market for Currencies in India

questions.explanation: Convertibility = ease with which a currency can be converted into other currencies and vice versa. India allows FULL convertibility on Current Account (trade in goods and services). India allows only PARTIAL convertibility on Capital Accounts (restrictions on capital flows). S.S. Tarapore and H.R. Khan committees recommended on convertibility of Indian Rupee.

questions.q: Which one of the following links all the ATMs in India?
questions.explanation: NPCI (National Payments Corporation of India) is the umbrella organization for all retail payment systems in India. Set up with guidance of RBI and IBA as a 'Not for Profit' organization under Section 8 of Companies Act 2013. Core objective: consolidate and integrate multiple systems into a nationwide uniform standard for all retail payment systems including ATMs. Ten core promoter banks include SBI, PNB, Canara Bank, BOB, Union Bank, BOI, ICICI, HDFC, Citibank and HSBC.

questions.q: Which one of the following statements correctly describes the meaning of legal tender money?

options.A: The money which is tendered in courts of law to defray the fee of legal cases
options.B: The money which a creditor is under compulsion to accept in settlement of his claims
options.C: The bank money in the form of cheques, drafts, bills of exchange, etc.
options.D: The metallic money in circulation in a country

questions.explanation: Legal tender is any official medium of payment recognized by law to extinguish public or private debt or meet a financial obligation. A creditor is obligated to accept legal tender toward repayment of a debt. Legal tender can only be issued by the national body authorized to do so.

questions.q: Which of the following Five-Year Plans was focussed on Human Resource Development?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: The SEVENTH Five-Year Plan (1985-90) was focused on Human Resource Development. 'Human resources development has necessarily to be assigned a key role in any development strategy.' PM: Rajiv Gandhi. Dr Manmohan Singh was Vice Chairman of Planning Commission from 1985-87. Target growth: 5%, Actual: 5.8%. Since none of the given options (Third, Fifth, First) is the Seventh Plan, the answer is E (None of the above).

questions.q: In which budget did the finance minister announce the Liberalized Exchange Rate Management System?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: In March 1992, the Indian government implemented LERMS (Liberalized Exchange Rate Management System). Dr Manmohan Singh announced it in his 1992 budget (Union Budget 1992-93). LERMS introduced partial convertibility — 40% of foreign exchange earnings surrendered at official rate, 60% at market-determined rate. Based on C. Rangarajan Committee recommendations.

questions.q:
Which of the following were goals of the Five-Year Plans?
1. Growth  2. Modernization  3. Self-reliance  4. Literature

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: The goals of the Five-Year Plans were: Growth, Modernisation, Self-reliance and Equity. Literature was NOT one of the objectives. All options A, B, C include 'Literature' which is wrong. The correct set (Growth, Modernisation, Self-reliance, Equity) is not listed as an option. Hence E (None of the above is a completely correct set).

questions.q: Which organization carries out the survey for determining the poverty line?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Poverty estimation is carried out by NITI Aayog's task force through poverty line calculation based on data captured by the National Sample Survey Office (NSSO) under MoSPI. NSSO was established in 1950. Key poverty committees: Alagh (1979), Lakdawala (1993), Tendulkar (2009), Rangarajan (2012). NSSO conducts the surveys; NITI Aayog uses the data.

questions.q: The trade balance of India (merchandise and services) for December 2022 (provisional) is

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: India's overall export (Merchandise and Services combined) was USD 61.82 Billion in December 2022 (negative growth of -5.26% YoY). Overall import was USD 73.80 Billion (negative growth of -1.95% YoY). Trade balance = 61.82 - 73.80 = -11.98 USD billion.

questions.q: What is the policy measure adopted by the Government of India to improve the system of agricultural marketing?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Agricultural marketing involves assembling, storage, processing, transportation, packaging, grading and distribution of agricultural commodities. Government policy measures include: (i) MSP assurance, (ii) maintenance of buffer stocks of wheat and rice by FCI, and (iii) distribution of food grains and sugar through PDS. All A, B and C are correct — answer is D.

questions.q: Which committee suggested the enactment of the Competition Act, 2002?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: The Monopolies and Restrictive Trade Practices Act, 1969 (MRTP Act) was repealed and replaced by the Competition Act, 2002 on recommendations of the S.V.S. Raghavan Committee. CCI was duly constituted in March 2009.

questions.q:
Consider the following statements:
i. RBI has the right to transact Government business of the Union of India
ii. State government transactions are carried out by RBI in terms of the agreement signed between them under the RBI Act, of 1934.
iii. The government of Sikkim and RBI have such an agreement.
iv. RBI cannot manage the public debt of the Union.
Select the correct statement using the code given below:

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Statement i is CORRECT: RBI has the right to transact Government business of the Union. Statement ii is CORRECT: State government transactions are carried out by RBI under the RBI Act 1934. Statement iii is INCORRECT: The Government of Sikkim does NOT have an agreement with RBI. Statement iv is INCORRECT: RBI can and does manage public debt of the Union. Hence only i and ii are correct.

questions.q: The apex financial institution for Agricultural and Rural Development in India is?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: NABARD (National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development) is the apex financial institution for Agricultural and Rural Development in India. Established on 12 July 1982 on recommendations of the B. Sivaraman Committee.

questions.q: Which among the following is the most optimal recovery?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Z-shaped recovery is the most optimal — the economy not only bounces back quickly but overshoots the pre-recession trend before settling back. V-shaped recovery is fast but just returns to the baseline. K-shaped recovery is uneven (different sectors recover differently).

questions.q: Electoral bonds can be purchased from:

options.A: Branches of State Bank of India
options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Electoral bonds can be purchased only from authorised branches of the State Bank of India (SBI). They are bearer instruments in the nature of a promissory note issued by SBI, redeemable only by eligible political parties.

questions.q:
With respect to the Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR), consider the following statements:
1. SLR is governed by the provisions of the Banking Regulation Act, of 1949
2. A reduction in the limit of SLR will increase bank credit.
3. State Development Loans do not qualify for SLR
Choose the correct Statements only

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Statement 1 is CORRECT: SLR is governed by the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. Statement 2 is CORRECT: Reduction in SLR allows banks to lend more, increasing bank credit. Statement 3 is INCORRECT: State Development Loans DO qualify for SLR. Hence statements 1 and 2 only are correct.

questions.q:
Lowering the Statutory Liquidity Ratio will have the following impact on the economy:
I. Banks will have higher leverage to liquidity.
II. The economy may see increased investment.
III. The supply of currency in the economy may broaden.
Select the correct code:

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Lowering SLR means banks maintain less proportion of deposits in liquid assets, giving them more funds to lend. This increases liquidity (I), enables more investment (II), and broadens the money supply (III). All three statements are correct.

questions.q: Which one of the following measures is likely to aid in improving India's Balance of Payment position?

options.C: Imposition of higher tariff on imports
options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: All three measures help improve India's BOP: Promotion of import substitution reduces import dependence (A). Devaluation discourages imports and encourages exports (B). Higher import tariffs reduce import volumes (C). Hence the answer is D (A, B, and C all apply).

questions.q: When the market value of goods and services at a constant price is greater than that of the Current price, then which of the following is correct:

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Real GDP is measured at constant prices (base year prices); Nominal GDP at current prices. If market value at constant prices (Real GDP) is greater than at current prices (Nominal GDP), current prices are lower than base year prices — a deflationary scenario. Hence Real GDP > Nominal GDP.
